cityEvents.name: Observatory Anomaly
cityEvents.description: A clock anomaly has made local news. The Observatory Keeper is stressed. The Watcher is more visible.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The meridian deviation is confirmed in the public record. Something has been loosened.

cityEvents.name: Park After Hours
cityEvents.description: Greenwich Park stays open past dark tonight. The park at night is at its most mythologically vivid.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The park boundary dissolves after dark. What is normally background becomes foreground.

cityEvents.name: Meridian Festival
cityEvents.description: Tourists crowd the meridian line. The player sees things in the crowd that nobody else does.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The line between worlds is also a line between east and west. Today it is crowded on both sides.

cityEvents.name: The Ship in the Dry Dock
cityEvents.description: The Cutty Sark behaves strangely. Rigging moves without wind. The hold sounds occupied.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: Eleven years on the sea, eleven years in glass. It remembers both.

cityEvents.name: Basin Arrival
cityEvents.description: A narrowboat arrived that wasn't expected. No registered owner. Eleven years on the canal without stopping. The Canal Boat Painter recognizes the paint.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The boat has been on every canal. It knows which ones are real.

cityEvents.name: St Anne's Night
cityEvents.description: The church opens for a night service for the first time in years. The interior is different at night.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The church at night is a different building. The daylight version is the cover.

cityEvents.name: Sound Installation
cityEvents.description: The Sound Artist has permission for a public installation at the basin. The public hears ambiance. The Musician hears the hidden layer.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The installation is tuned to the canal frequency. The Sound Artist knows exactly what he is amplifying.

cityEvents.name: The Night Market Expansion
cityEvents.description: The market appears on a night it shouldn't. Nobody organized it.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The market runs on a schedule that predates the market.

cityEvents.name: New Mural
cityEvents.description: A mural appeared overnight on a railway arch. Nobody saw it painted. It depicts something mythologically significant.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The paint is dry. It has always been dry.

cityEvents.name: Market Dawn
cityEvents.description: The antiques market has a pre-dawn hour where different objects appear — objects from the foundation layer.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: Before the market opens to the public, it opens to something else.

cityEvents.name: Gallery After Dark
cityEvents.description: The gallery opens at midnight. By invitation. The art is different at night.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The gallery shows what it wants during the day. At midnight it shows what it has.

cityEvents.name: Warehouse Auction
cityEvents.description: The warehouse contents are supposedly being auctioned. The objects on offer don't match what's inside. The Night Guard says one word: 'Wrong.'
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The auction is a cover. The real transfer happens in the objects that aren't listed.

cityEvents.name: The Fox Trail
cityEvents.description: The Night Fox appears in all three neighborhoods tonight, leaving a visible trail. Following it reveals something at each stop.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The fox knows the route. It has always known the route.

cityEvents.name: The Thames Surge
cityEvents.description: Unusually high tide. The foreshore is submerged. But in Limehouse basin, the water is lower than expected. Something is pulling it.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The Thames moves in two directions simultaneously today. One of them is not downstream.

cityEvents.name: The Walking Figure
cityEvents.description: The Thames Path Figure is seen by multiple NPCs across all neighborhoods. The Watcher is agitated.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The Figure and the Watcher have not been in the same place before. Something has changed.

cityEvents.name: All Clocks
cityEvents.description: Every clock in Greenwich shows a different time today. The times, when plotted, form a sequence.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The sequence spells something. The Observatory Keeper has not come in today.

cityEvents.name: The Chalk Map
cityEvents.description: The Child Who Draws has covered an entire pavement section overnight. The map shows all three neighborhoods. And a fourth.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The fourth neighborhood has no name in any directory. The streets are real.

cityEvents.name: Network Pulse
cityEvents.description: Every NPC mentions the same word today. Different contexts. Same word. The word is the first syllable of a name.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The barista hears it first. She always hears first.

cityEvents.name: Deep Tremor
cityEvents.description: A tremor felt only in specific locations. Not seismic — felt in the hands, not the feet. The foundation marks are warm.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The shopkeeper's hands know the difference. They have been keeping this for years.

cityEvents.name: Signal Spike
cityEvents.description: The canal frequency spikes for 24 hours. The recording, at half speed, contains a word.
cityEvents.mythologicalNote: The word is not in English. It is not in any language with a current speaker.
